verdict: The rhythm is perfectly synchronized to the 140 BPM tempo, but the physical flow is severely compromised by frequent vertical 16th-note jumps and awkward spins.

issues.problem: Frequent 16th-note transitions between Down and Up (or vice versa) force extremely awkward vertical double-steps or rapid hip twists.
The 16th-note sequence D -> R -> U -> D forms a full spin at 140 BPM, which is highly disorienting and physically awkward to play.
The 16th-note run U -> L -> R -> D -> U creates a highly complex, uncomfortable sequence of double-crossovers.
